compiled_state.title: Contract: CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN confidence signal emit/parse interface
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description
Create the contract document defining the CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN confidence signal interface between implementation sub-agents (emitter) and the sprint orchestrator (parser).

## Implementation
Create plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md with:
- Signal Name: CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN
- Emitter: Implementation sub-agents (via task-execution.md prompt)
- Parser: Sprint orchestrator (Phase 5 post-batch processing)
- Fields:
  - CONFIDENT — single keyword, no payload. Indicates agent has high confidence in task completion.
  - UNCERTAIN:<reason> — keyword followed by colon and free-text reason. Indicates agent lacks confidence.
- Output position: emitted as a line in the sub-agent's final output, alongside STATUS: and FILES_MODIFIED: lines
- Example payloads for both signals
- Tracking semantics: UNCERTAIN count tracked per story (not per task ID) to prevent counter reset on task replacement

## test-exempt: no conditional logic
This is a documentation-only contract artifact with no executable behavior. Any test would be a change-detector test asserting string presence.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contract file exists at expected path
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md
- [ ] Contract defines both CONFIDENT and UNCERTAIN signal formats
  Verify: grep -q 'CONFIDENT' plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md && grep -q 'UNCERTAIN' plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md
- [ ] Contract specifies emitter and parser components
  Verify: grep -q 'Emitter' plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md && grep -q 'Parser' plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh

comments.body: AC amendment (gap analysis — implicit_assumption): Contract must define interaction between CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN and existing STATUS:pass|fail. Key clarifications needed: (1) STATUS:pass + UNCERTAIN is valid (agent is uncertain about correctness despite task passing), (2) STATUS:fail does not automatically imply UNCERTAIN — they are orthogonal, (3) only UNCERTAIN signals on STATUS:pass tasks count toward the double-failure threshold (STATUS:fail already triggers revert-to-open in Phase 5 Step 9).
comments.author: Main

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Validation failed — all failures are pre-existing system-level fork resource exhaustion (EAGAIN errno 35), not related to this doc-only change
comments.author: Main

comments.body: Fixed: Contract for CONFIDENT/UNCERTAIN confidence signal created at plugins/dso/docs/contracts/confidence-signal.md
comments.author: Main
